_meta.purpose: Genre conventions for automated game-generation pipeline. Each genre defines what the engine MUST emit (controls, scenes, HUD, sounds, mechanics) so a generated game is recognizable as that genre.

identity.summary: Turn-based grid tactics. The defining property is discrete turns separated by an explicit end-turn action, with units occupying tiles on a 2D grid (square or hex). Adjacent genres distinguished by: real-time strategy lacks the turn boundary; tactical RPG centers on a small named party with leveling rather than faction-scale resource pipelines; 4X adds the macro layer of empire (cities, tech tree, diplomacy) on top of unit movement.
must_not_have: continuous time advance during active play
pixel-perfect collision (grid cells own the game)

scheme: Left-click on unit -> select; show movement range + attack range overlays
Left-click on highlighted tile -> issue move/attack/ability
Right-click -> deselect / cancel pending order
Spacebar or End-Turn button -> commit turn
Tab -> cycle to next unit with actions remaining
Mouse wheel / WASD -> camera pan when map exceeds viewport
Hover -> tooltip showing unit stats, terrain bonus, hit chance
controls.telegraphing: Per Into the Breach GDC 2019: enemies show their next-turn intent BEFORE the player commits. This is core to modern tactics design and should be the default for generated games.

player_actions: attack (melee adjacent or ranged with line-of-sight)
build (in 4X / Advance Wars: produce unit at factory/city)
research (in 4X: spend science on tech node)
win_conditions: domination: all enemy faction units/cities destroyed
objective: capture N specific tiles (Advance Wars HQ capture)
wonder: complete a long build chain (4X)
score: highest score when turn limit reached
survival: survive N turns (Into the Breach: protect grid power)
lose_conditions: key objective destroyed (e.g., HQ captured, power grid drained in Into the Breach)
turn limit exceeded without meeting win condition
unit-permadeath cascade (XCOM: lose enough soldiers, run ends)

scene_structure.WorldMap: primary battle scene with grid, units, fog of war
scene_structure.UnitDetail: modal showing stats / abilities for selected unit
scene_structure.EndOfTurn: AI moves animate sequentially; UI input disabled
scene_structure.Defeat: loss screen, retry / back to menu

standard_unit_types: siege / artillery (high damage, long range, slow)
wizard / hero / commander (special abilities, often 1 per side)

resources.command_points: spent on CO powers (Advance Wars / XCOM-style)

hud_elements: minimap (bottom-left or top-right with fog overlay)
selected_unit_panel (bottom: portrait, HP, action points, abilities)
enemy_intent_arrows (Into the Breach: arrow showing next-turn attack)
tooltip (on hover, terrain + unit + hit chance)
iconic_mechanics: fog_of_war: tiles outside vision are dark or remembered-but-stale
zones_of_control: enemy units freeze movement of adjacent friendlies
terrain_bonuses: forest +cover, hill +range, road +movement
line_of_sight: ranged attacks blocked by walls/forest
permadeath: lost units do not return (XCOM)
telegraphed_threats: enemy intent shown next turn before player commits (Into the Breach)
fog_lift_on_kill: killing scout reveals area
captures: infantry standing on tile for N turns flips ownership
sound_design: unit_move (footstep loop or vehicle rumble, gated to movement length)
attack (per-weapon: rifle pop, sword swing, missile launch)
common_bugs: TURN_REENTRY: end-turn handler triggers on stale state and cascades 5+ turns in a single frame. Cause: not gating input or using a state machine flag. Fix: explicit turn_state ENUM (PLAYER_TURN / RESOLVING / AI_TURN / RESOLVING / NEXT_TURN) and disable end-turn button during RESOLVING.
UNIT_DUPLICATION: save/load creates duplicate sprites for the same unit ID. Cause: spawning from save without clearing prior scene state. Fix: serialize by id, clear sprite cache on scene restart.
AI_INFINITE_LOOP: AI considers same unit twice in same turn. Fix: mark each AI unit acted=true and skip; cap thinking time per unit.
MOVEMENT_PATH_DESYNC: visual sprite arrives at wrong tile because pathfinding ran on grid but tween ran on pixels. Fix: snap sprite to tile center on path complete.
ZOC_MISS: enemy unit attacks through a zone-of-control zone because adjacency check ignored ZoC layer. Fix: ZoC is a separate validation step in move resolver.
FOG_BLEED: unit visible through fog because vision was checked against unit position only, not the path it walked. Fix: re-evaluate vision after each tile of a multi-tile move.
OFF_BY_ONE_RANGE: 'range 3' actually allows range 4 because Chebyshev vs Manhattan distance was mixed.
DEAD_UNIT_RETURNS: unit killed mid-turn still on action queue. Fix: filter dead units before AI iteration.

works: Phaser.Tilemaps.Tilemap with a single static layer for terrain + a dynamic layer for unit overlay
GameObject Group per faction so AI iteration is one loop
Pointer events on tile coords (worldToTileXY) not on sprite hit areas
Tween chain for AI move animations with onComplete -> next_unit
Camera bounds set to map extents, drag-to-pan with this.input.on('pointermove')
Container per unit holding sprite + healthbar + status icons; one transform updates them together
Easystar.js or A* for pathfinding on a JS grid mirror of tilemap
avoid: Arcade physics for unit movement (overkill, causes pixel drift)
Per-tile sprite for terrain (use tilemap layer instead, 100x faster)
Synchronous AI thinking on update() loop (blocks render)
Storing unit state on the sprite itself (separate model+view)
Phaser.Geom.Polygon for fog of war (use a RenderTexture mask instead)
phaser_implementation.scene_skeleton: BootScene -> MenuScene -> WorldMapScene (main) with parallel UIScene overlay for HUD, EndOfTurnScene as modal

identity.summary: Real-time management. The game advances on a fixed-rate simulation tick (decoupled from render framerate), and the player's primary verb is placement/zoning/scheduling rather than direct unit control. Distinguished from strategy by absence of explicit turns; from action games by absence of avatar control (or the avatar is a thin wrapper around economy actions).
must_have: fixed-rate sim tick independent of FPS
stat decay over time (need to refill)

scheme: Left-click on palette item -> enter build mode for that item
Left-click on world tile -> place item
Left-click + drag -> zone or path placement (roads, fields)
Right-click -> cancel build mode / deselect / interact
Mouse wheel -> zoom
WASD or middle-drag -> pan camera
Number keys 1/2/3 -> game speed (pause/normal/fast)
Spacebar -> pause/unpause
Tab or M -> toggle map / overlay layer (heatmap, pollution, happiness)

player_actions: trade (buy/sell with caravan or world market)
research (Two Point: unlock new room types)
win_conditions: scenario_objective_met (e.g., reach 50k population, hit profit target by year 5)
happiness_milestone (citizens averaged at X for N days)
campaign_chapter_complete (Two Point: stars rating)
endless: no win, score-driven (SimCity sandbox, RimWorld)
lose_conditions: bankruptcy (cash below threshold for N consecutive ticks)
scenario_failure (failed to meet milestone by deadline)

scene_structure.BuildModeOverlay: palette UI shown when build active (sub-state of PlayScene, not separate scene)
scene_structure.InfoOverlays: heatmap toggles for pollution / happiness / traffic / power (mask layers)
scene_structure.ResearchTree: modal for tech progression (Two Point Hospital)
scene_structure.CitizenInspector: click on pawn -> modal with needs, schedule, traits

standard_entity_types: house / dwelling (population produces workers, consumes food/water/power)
farm / resource_extractor (produces raw resource on tick)
factory / processor (consumes raw + worker, produces goods)
road / path (connects buildings, enables citizen pathing)
citizen / pawn (the simulated agent: needs, job, mood)
vehicle (truck, ambulance, taxi: spawns from building, traverses roads)

resources.money: primary currency, drained by upkeep and gained by taxes/sales
resources.energy_power: produced by plants, consumed by buildings; outage cascades
resources.water: similar to power, gates housing growth
resources.happiness_satisfaction: aggregate of citizen moods, gates growth + triggers riots
resources.pollution: negative side effect of industry; reduces nearby happiness
resources.raw_materials: wood, stone, iron — supply chain inputs

hud_elements: alert_feed (right side: 'fire on Elm St', 'citizen left town')
iconic_mechanics: tick_based_simulation: world updates every fixed delta (e.g., 100ms) regardless of render FPS. Per Satisfactory factory tick model.
supply_chain: building A produces good X, building B requires X within delivery range
stat_decay: citizens lose hunger/sleep/comfort each tick; must be refilled at appropriate building
happiness_radius: parks/decorations boost happiness within N tiles
pollution_radius: factories drop happiness within N tiles
RCI_demand (residential/commercial/industrial): zoned tiles fill based on demand bars
pathing_simulation: citizens walk roads from home to work to shop, taking time
disaster_events: random fires, disease, raids force reactive play
speed_control: pause to think, fast-forward to skip dead time
save_anywhere: state must serialize cleanly mid-tick
AI_storyteller: RimWorld-style event director balances pressure
common_bugs: TICK_DESYNC: sim tick coupled to delta time, so faster machines simulate faster. Fix: accumulator pattern, fixed timestep, render with interpolation. Per Handmade Network and gamedev.net consensus.
FRAMERATE_TIED_PHYSICS: citizen movement uses delta directly, so 144Hz monitor moves them faster. Fix: separate sim update from render update.
SAVE_STATE_INTEGRITY: serialization mid-build leaves orphan ghost building. Fix: pause sim during save, atomic write.
PATHFINDING_STALE: citizen path computed at spawn never re-evaluated; bulldozed road leaves citizen walking through air. Fix: re-path on graph change.
FLOATING_POINT_DRIFT: long sessions accumulate floating errors in stat decay. Fix: integer ticks for sim, float only for render interpolation.
OVERLAPPING_BUILDS: two buildings placed on same tile via race condition. Fix: tile-occupancy guard before placement commit.
MEMORY_LEAK_SPRITES: bulldozed buildings leak sprite refs. Fix: explicit destroy() and pool reuse.
ALERT_SPAM: same fire reported every tick. Fix: dedupe alerts by source+type with cooldown.
BANKRUPTCY_OSCILLATION: cash dips to negative for one tick, triggers game over even though next tick recovers. Fix: require N consecutive ticks below threshold.
ZONE_FLOOD_FILL: drag-zone fills wrong region because flood-fill ran on stale grid.

works: Custom fixed-tick scheduler: this.simAccumulator += delta; while (simAccumulator >= TICK_MS) { tick(); simAccumulator -= TICK_MS; }
Tilemap layer for ground; separate dynamic-tilemap or sprite-group layer for buildings
Citizens as Container with sprite + label; pooled via Phaser.GameObjects.Group with maxSize
Heatmap overlays as RenderTexture or BitmapData updated every N ticks (not every frame)
RexUI or custom HTML overlay for build palette + alert feed
Pathfinding with Easystar.js on a numeric grid mirror, run on web worker for large maps
Camera with setBounds + drag-pan + mouse-wheel zoom
avoid: Arcade physics for citizen movement (overkill and tied to render delta)
Per-frame stat updates (do them on tick, interpolate visually)
Phaser timers for the main sim clock (drift); use accumulator on update()
Storing sim state in scene properties only (use a plain JS model object so save/load is JSON.stringify)
phaser_implementation.scene_skeleton: BootScene -> MenuScene -> PlayScene + UIScene overlay; modals (CitizenInspector, ResearchTree) as paused-PlayScene with launched scene

identity.summary: Single-board problem-solving with discrete moves toward a goal state. The defining property is that every state is reproducible from start + move list, and the goal is a recognizable winning configuration. Distinguished from strategy by absence of opponents (or a degenerate solo opponent like falling Tetrominoes); from sim by no time decay between player actions (Tetris is a hybrid).
must_not_have: twitch reflex beyond optional time pressure

by_subgenre.match3 (Bejeweled, Candy Crush): click-then-click adjacent OR click-and-drag swap; pointerdown -> pointerup direction = swap axis
by_subgenre.tile_pusher (Sokoban): arrow keys / WASD / swipe; one cell per input; double-tap or Z for undo
by_subgenre.drop_block (Tetris): left/right arrow for horizontal, up or X/Z for rotate, down for soft-drop, space for hard-drop
by_subgenre.logic_grid (Picross, Sudoku): left-click fill, right-click flag/cross, number keys for digits
by_subgenre.physics_puzzle (Portal-style 2D): WASD move + mouse aim + click to use tool
by_subgenre.draw_path (Flow Free): click and drag to draw connection
by_subgenre.rotate_pipe: click on tile to rotate 90 degrees

player_actions: push (Sokoban: walk into block adjacent to empty)
connect (Flow Free: draw line between matching dots)
use_hint (consume hint resource to highlight valid move)
win_conditions: board_clear (Bejeweled: clear N gems / Tetris: lines per level)
all_targets_filled (Sokoban: every box on a goal tile)
configuration_match (Picross: revealed image matches solution)
score_threshold (reach N points before move limit)
puzzle_solved (Sudoku: all cells filled validly)
all_pieces_placed (jigsaw)
complete_N_puzzles (campaign completion)
lose_conditions: deadlock (Sokoban: pushed box into corner; usually allows reset)

scene_structure.LevelSelect: grid of unlocked levels with star ratings

standard_piece_types_by_subgenre.match3: 5 to 7 colored gems + 2 to 3 power-ups (line bomb, color bomb, area blast)
standard_piece_types_by_subgenre.tetris: 7 standard tetrominoes (I, O, T, S, Z, J, L)
standard_piece_types_by_subgenre.sokoban: player, wall, floor, box, goal_tile, box_on_goal
standard_piece_types_by_subgenre.picross: filled cell, marked-empty cell, unknown cell, row/column number clue
standard_piece_types_by_subgenre.sudoku: digits 1 to 9, given vs entered cells
standard_piece_types_by_subgenre.flow_free: endpoint dots in N colors, drawn path segments
standard_piece_types_by_subgenre.portal_2d: player, cube, button, door, portal_orange, portal_blue, hazard

resources.stars: 1 to 3 per level, persists across runs

hud_elements: objective_panel ('clear 20 blue gems' / 'reach goal in 15 moves')
iconic_mechanics: undo_redo: per Phaser Sokoban tutorial — Command Pattern, store inverse of each move; double-tap or dedicated button
hint_system: precomputed solver indicates a valid next move
chain_combos: match3 cascades when falling pieces form new matches
level_progression: solve N to unlock N+1; star rating per level
deterministic_replay: same seed + same inputs = same game (essential for daily challenge / leaderboard)
telegraphing_failures: Sokoban shows when box is dead-stuck (wall corner)
soft_lock_detection: Tetris top-out, Sokoban deadlock — explicit fail-state check
tutorialization: first 3 levels teach one mechanic each
sound_design: match_or_correct (bright affirm chime, often pitched up per combo)
line_clear (Tetris: longer chime, scaled to lines cleared)
music_loop (calm, looping, tempo can rise on near-fail)
common_bugs: UNDO_STATE_CORRUPTION: undo restores moving pieces but not chain state (e.g., undo after a Bejeweled cascade leaves missing gems). Fix: snapshot full board state on each move OR store full inverse including post-cascade resolution. Phaser Sokoban tutorial explicitly addresses this: only restore moving entities, but for cascade games full snapshot is required.
CLICK_HITBOX_OFF_BY_ONE: pointer between two cells registers on wrong cell because hit area is 32px but tile is 33px. Fix: align hit area exactly with tile bounds, use floor(x/tileWidth).
DOUBLE_INPUT: rapid click registers as two swaps. Fix: input lockout while animation in progress (this.busy=true).
ANIMATION_RACE: piece dropped mid-swap-animation breaks board state. Fix: queue all input during animations, gate on tween onComplete.
MATCH_DETECTION_MISSED: 4-in-a-row not detected because scan only checked 3-in-a-row patterns. Fix: scan from longest match to shortest, or run length-encoding pass per row+column.
DEADLOCK_NOT_DETECTED: Sokoban lets player play after impossible state. Fix: deadlock detector after each push, surface as 'No more solutions — undo or reset?'
TETROMINO_KICK_FAIL: rotation against wall fails when SRS wall-kick should allow it. Fix: implement SRS table.
SCORE_OVERFLOW: long-run integer overflow on combo multiplier. Fix: cap multiplier or use BigInt.
STARS_REGRESSION: replaying with worse score overwrites better score. Fix: store max(old, new).
RNG_NOT_SEEDED: same level plays differently between sessions. Fix: seed RNG from level number for reproducibility.

works: Logical board as 2D JS array; sprites are visual mirror only — keep the model authoritative
Match3 swap: pointerdown stores origin tile, pointerup measures direction (>32px on axis), validates adjacency, applies swap with tween
Tween chain for cascades: gravity tween onComplete -> match scan -> if matches, tween clear -> repeat
Sokoban: cursor keys via this.input.keyboard.on('keydown-LEFT'), gate on !this.busy
Tetris: timer event for gravity tick; rotate via Phaser tween or instant snap
Object pooling for gems/pieces (Group with createMultiple) — reuse on cascade
Tweens.timeline for sequenced animations (clear -> drop -> refill)
avoid: Putting board state on sprites (sprites are presentation; model is source of truth)
Using Arcade physics for falling gems (too imprecise; use tween to row Y)
Skipping the lockout flag — simultaneous inputs corrupt state
Dynamic tilemap for the puzzle grid (pieces move per cell — sprite group is simpler and cleaner)
phaser_implementation.scene_skeleton: BootScene -> MenuScene -> LevelSelectScene -> PuzzleScene; WinModal and PauseModal as launched overlay scenes

identity.summary: Digital adaptation of a tabletop board game. The defining property is fidelity to physical rules with rule-enforcement automation: the digital version enforces what humans would otherwise check (legal moves, scoring, turn order). Distinguished from strategy by smaller and tighter rule sets, often a fixed board (or modular but shuffled once), and per-turn phases that are explicit and named in the rulebook.
must_have: faithful turn structure with explicit phases
must_not_have: real-time elements (the genre IS turn-based by definition)
fog of war for fully open-info games like Chess (vs hidden-info games like Codenames)
departures from physical rules without an explicit 'house rules' option

scheme: Left-click on piece -> select; legal destinations highlight
Click-and-drag piece to destination, OR click destination to confirm
Drag card from hand to play area
Right-click -> cancel / inspect (zoom card)
Per-phase action buttons (Roll Dice, End Turn, Pass)
Hover -> rulebook tooltip on game element
Chat / emote panel for online multiplayer

common: place_piece (Catan settlement, Chess move, Azul tile)
trade (Catan: propose to other players, bank trade 4:1 / 3:1 / 2:1)
draft_pick (Azul: take all of one color from a factory)

examples.Catan: first to 10 victory points (settlements, cities, longest road, largest army, dev cards)
examples.Azul: highest score after a round ends with someone completing a horizontal row
examples.Codenames: team identifies all their agents before opposing team OR opposing team picks the assassin
examples.Chess: checkmate opposing king OR opponent resigns OR time forfeit
examples.generic: reach point threshold OR last player standing OR specific objective complete

scene_structure.Menu: new game / join / how to play
scene_structure.Setup: deal cards, place initial pieces (e.g., Catan starting settlements)
scene_structure.ActionPhasePanel: per-phase UI shown contextually (Catan: roll -> trade -> build; Codenames: clue -> guess)
scene_structure.ScoreBoard: side panel or modal showing each player's score

by_game.Catan: wood, brick, sheep, wheat, ore, victory_points
by_game.Azul: tiles by color, score, broken-tiles penalty

hud_elements: current_player_indicator (whose turn, with timer in online play)
score_panel (each player's points, current leader highlighted)
history_log ('P1 built a settlement at H3' — feed of every action)
iconic_mechanics: phased_turns: each turn has named phases the player progresses through (Catan: Roll -> Trade -> Build), with a button to advance phase
rule_enforcement: cannot make illegal moves — UI greys out invalid options
hidden_information: cards in hand visible only to owner; Codenames key_card visible only to spymasters
deterministic_with_RNG: shuffled deck order is deterministic from seed; replays possible
trading_interface: structured proposal form (Catan: 'I offer X for Y from any player')
tableau_building: cards/tokens accumulate in personal play area
drafting: pick from a shared pool (Azul factories)
scoring_animation: end-of-round tallies highlight each scoring rule
asynchronous_play: online games may allow long turn timers (24h)
AI_opponents: rule-bound bots with difficulty levels
common_bugs: ILLEGAL_MOVE_ALLOWED: rule check missed an edge case (Catan: settlement placed within 2 of another). Fix: comprehensive legal-move generator, used both for highlighting AND for validation, single source of truth.
PHASE_SKIP: 'End Turn' clickable during build phase before resources subtracted. Fix: phase state machine with explicit transitions; disable buttons not valid for current phase.
HIDDEN_INFO_LEAK: opponent's cards visible due to client-side rendering of full state. Fix: server-authoritative state; clients only receive what they should see.
TURN_DESYNC_ONLINE: client and server disagree on whose turn. Fix: server is authoritative; clients reconcile on every action ack.
RECONNECT_LOSES_HAND: rejoining player loses card knowledge. Fix: persist per-player view on server, replay on reconnect.
AI_TIMEOUT: bot thinks too long, blocks game. Fix: hard time cap with fallback to random legal move.
TIE_BREAK_NOT_HANDLED: two players reach 10 VP simultaneously. Fix: explicit tiebreak rules per game (Catan: most resources; Azul: most rows; Chess: not possible).
UNDO_VS_RULES: undo allowed in single-player but breaks online competitive integrity. Fix: undo only with opponent consent.
DRAG_CANCEL_KEEPS_PIECE_OFFBOARD: aborted drag leaves piece visually disconnected. Fix: snap-back animation on dragend if invalid.
SHUFFLE_NOT_RANDOM: deterministic shuffle by accident reveals card order. Fix: cryptographically seeded shuffle, server-side.

works: Plain JS state object as authoritative source; Phaser scene reads and renders from it
Pointer events with hitarea per board cell / card / piece
this.input.setDraggable(piece) + dragstart/drag/dragend events for click+drag movement
Containers per player area (hand, board, score panel) for organized layout
Modals as launched scenes layered on the play scene
Tweens for piece movement, dice roll bounce, card flip
RexUI plugin (or simple sprite buttons) for context action panels
Color matrix or tint for player-color theming
avoid: Storing game state on sprites (sprite is view; logical game is the model)
Phaser Arcade Physics (irrelevant for board games)
Per-piece sprite update on every frame (only redraw on state change)
Hardcoding rules in UI handlers — keep rules in a Rules class so headless tests can run them
phaser_implementation.scene_skeleton: BootScene -> MenuScene -> LobbyScene (multiplayer) -> SetupScene -> PlayScene + UIScene; modals (CardZoom, Rulebook, ScoreBoard, EndOfRound) as overlay scenes
phaser_implementation.headless_test_path: Rules class must be Phaser-free so unit tests can verify legal moves, scoring, and win detection without rendering

identity.summary: Action RPG with 3D rendering and isometric or over-the-shoulder camera. The defining property over 2D ARPG is 3D mesh-based rendering, depth sorting, and (typically) free camera rotation; the gameplay loop (click-to-move, click-to-attack, loot drops, skill bar) is shared with 2D ARPG. In Phaser/web context this is achieved with isometric 2.5D tilemaps and depth sorting rather than full 3D. Distinguished from full 3D action games by fixed-angle (or limited-rotation) camera and grid-snapped pathfinding under a 3D-rendered surface.
must_have: depth sorting (entities behind objects occluded correctly)

scheme: Left-click on ground -> move
Left-click on enemy -> auto-engage (move into range, attack)
Left-click on item -> pick up
Right-click -> cast skill bound to right mouse OR alt-fire
Number keys 1 to 6 -> activate skill from skill bar
Q / E -> rotate camera (if rotatable) OR potion / dodge
Tab -> toggle inventory
C -> toggle character sheet
M -> toggle map
Spacebar -> dodge or jump
Mouse wheel -> zoom in/out (if allowed)

win_conditions: story_complete (campaign final boss defeated)
act_complete (chapter milestone)
endless_progression (Diablo: greater rifts, no win, just power scaling)
objective_quest (clear N waves, kill boss in level)
lose_conditions: character_death (HP to 0; respawn at checkpoint OR hardcore: permadeath)

scene_structure.TownHub: social area, vendors, stash, no enemies
scene_structure.InventoryOverlay: modal for gear management (often pauses game)

resources.health_hp: primary survival resource, regenerates or via potion
resources.mana_or_resource: skill cost (mana, fury, energy, spirit — class-specific)
resources.skill_points: earned per level, spent in skill tree

hud_elements: skill_bar (center bottom: 4 to 8 slots with cooldown overlays)
loot_labels (tab to highlight, color-coded by rarity)
iconic_mechanics: click_to_move_pathfinding: navmesh or grid A* under 3D mesh
depth_sorting_isometric: y-coord (or world-z) sorts draw order so trees/walls occlude correctly. Per Phaser isometric tutorial: cartesian-to-iso transform + depth = y + z * 1000
loot_explosion: enemies drop items in radial burst
loot_rarity_tiers: white/blue/yellow/orange/green color coding (Diablo standard)
skill_cooldowns: each skill has individual recharge
level_scaling: zone monsters scale with player level (or fixed difficulty bands)
checkpoints / waypoints: fast travel between unlocked points
boss_telegraphs: AoE indicators show where attacks will land
gear_affixes: random stat suffixes (+5 fire dmg, +10% movement)
set_bonuses: wearing N pieces of a set grants combined bonus
respawn_with_gear_loss: hardcore vs softcore modes
town_portal: return to safe zone from anywhere
sound_design: combat_music (intensifies in combat, fades out after)
common_bugs: PATHFINDING_FALLS_THROUGH_FLOOR: 3D collision with stairs / sloped terrain; navmesh boundary missed. Fix: navmesh stitched at transitions, fallback ground-snap.
DEPTH_SORTING_WRONG: tall tree drawn behind player when player is in front. Fix: depth = y + z bias; recompute every frame for moving entities.
ENTITY_FLICKER: two entities at same y oscillate draw order each frame. Fix: stable secondary sort key (entity ID).
CAMERA_CLIPS_GEOMETRY: camera goes inside wall on rotation. Fix: camera collision sphere, push out.
AOE_HITBOX_2D_ON_3D: skill targets pixel position not world position. Fix: transform pointer to world coords with iso-to-cartesian inverse.
LOOT_INSIDE_WALL: dropped item lands in geometry. Fix: validate drop position against navmesh before spawn.
INVENTORY_DUPE: drag-and-drop race creates duplicate item. Fix: server-authoritative inventory, atomic move.
SKILL_INFINITE_COMBO: cooldown not started because skill canceled. Fix: cooldown starts on cast intent, not on completion.
MEMORY_LEAK_PROJECTILES: projectiles not despawned after timeout. Fix: TTL on every projectile.
LEVEL_UP_DURING_DEATH: kill XP counted after death animation, level-up triggers on dead character. Fix: gate level-up on alive==true.
RESPAWN_LOOP: respawn point inside enemy pack causes instant re-death. Fix: respawn invuln window + safe-position check.

phaser_implementation.note: Phaser is 2D, but isometric 2.5D achieves the 3D-ARPG feel. For true 3D consider three.js + a separate ECS layer.
works: Isometric tilemap with diamond-shape tiles (32x16 or 64x32) and cartesian-to-iso transform
Depth sorting: scene.children.sort by gameObject.y for ground entities; static occluders given fixed depth
Easystar.js or A* for click-to-move on a flat 2D grid mirror, mapped back to iso for visuals
Particle emitters for attack / spell impacts
RenderTexture for fog of war (dark layer with cleared circle around player)
Container per entity bundling sprite + healthbar + name label + status icons
Skill cooldowns via simple { skillId, readyAt } map; UI reads timestamp per frame
Loot as pooled sprite group with rarity tinting
avoid: Trying to do real 3D in Phaser (use three.js for that)
Per-frame depth sort on hundreds of entities with full sort (use insertion sort on near-sorted list, or Phaser's built-in displayList sort)
Storing entity state on sprites only (use ECS or plain model objects so combat math is testable)
Pixel-perfect collision (use grid-cell or circle hitboxes; pixel-perfect is too expensive)
Flat top-down sprites in iso world without 8-directional facing
phaser_implementation.scene_skeleton: BootScene -> MainMenu -> CharCreation -> TownHub <-> PlayScene; UIScene overlay (skillbar, globes, minimap); InventoryOverlay + SkillTreeOverlay as launched scenes that pause PlayScene
